baseline_comparison.summary: No headline task produced a fully comparable non-single-class diagnosis_anchor result, so there is no meaningful baseline race to call.

benchmark_method.metric_caveat: Balanced accuracy is computed over the classes observed in each evaluation split. On single-class validation or test splits it collapses to observed-class recall and should not be treated as strong separability evidence.
benchmark_method.missing_value_policy: Rows with no substantive family features are skipped. Remaining blank feature values are zero-filled within the selected family columns for deterministic distance calculations.

claim_boundary_statement: Representation artifacts extend cross-sectional comparison support only. They do not upgrade the repo beyond narrow-go or narrow_outcome_benchmark, and they do not constitute model benchmarking.

context_only_tasks.label_caveat: This cohort is tiny and family-structured. It is context only and should not be treated as headline evidence for schizophrenia separability.
context_only_tasks.label_definition: schizophrenia versus pooled sibling/control family-context labels within ds000115

context_only_tasks.label_definition: broad psychiatric patient versus general population control within TCP

current_limitations: ucla-cnp-ds000030 and ds000115 remain cross-sectional-only cohorts with zero outcome rows.
tcp-ds005237 remains label-limited and does not support psychosis-specific equivalence.
These artifacts standardize public measures conservatively for representation comparison only; no predictive claim is implied.

headline_tasks.label_caveat: This remains the strongest current strict-open psychosis/control task, but the frozen split contract is still concurrent-only and extremely small.
headline_tasks.label_definition: psychosis versus control within the public FEP cross-sectional cohort

headline_tasks.label_caveat: This task is useful for representation comparison, but it remains transdiagnostic context rather than a stronger psychosis-specific or outcome claim.
headline_tasks.label_definition: schizophrenia versus pooled non-schizophrenia context labels within UCLA CNP

skipped_families.symptom_profile: No labeled test rows with usable feature support.

rationale: fep-ds003944 / psychosis_vs_control has no evaluable holdout comparison under the frozen split contract.
ucla-cnp-ds000030 / schizophrenia_vs_non_schizophrenia_context only yields single-class holdout rows, so it does not provide meaningful headline evidence.
There are no fully comparable non-single-class headline task/split pairs for a meaningful diagnosis_anchor baseline race.
recommendation.summary: Headline comparisons do not clear the diagnosis_anchor baseline on fully comparable non-single-class holdouts. Keep the repo at the descriptive artifact layer rather than pulling model comparison forward.

tasks.label_caveat: This remains the strongest current strict-open psychosis/control task, but the frozen split contract is still concurrent-only and extremely small.
